chunks.title: Bio
chunks.text:
Bio

Mihir Makwana is a computer vision and deep learning engineer based in London, UK. Mihir is currently pursuing an MSc in Artificial Intelligence at Kingston University, London, with a professional placement pathway. Mihir's background combines current AI study with one year of production full-stack work, so he can move comfortably between model development, application wiring, and deployment-minded delivery.

At the time of this portfolio, Mihir is seeking AI/ML internships, placements, and part-time roles in London. Mihir's public contact details in the CV are `mihirpmakwana786@gmail.com`, LinkedIn, GitHub, and a live portfolio site. The strongest recurring themes in Mihir's work are practical computer vision, end-to-end web delivery, and turning coursework or prototypes into something demonstrable.

MSc Artificial Intelligence, Kingston University, London

Mihir is currently studying MSc Artificial Intelligence at Kingston University, London. The program runs from January 2026 to January 2028 and includes a Professional Placement year. Mihir's current focus is applied AI, especially computer vision and deep learning, which aligns with the projects shown in this portfolio.

chunks.title: Master of Computer Applications (MCA), ISTAR College, Gujarat
chunks.text:
Master of Computer Applications (MCA), ISTAR College, Gujarat

Mihir completed his MCA at ISTAR College, Gujarat, from July 2023 to May 2025, graduating with a CGPA of 9.27/10. The course work listed in the CV includes React, Node.js, Laravel, MongoDB, and Python. Mihir also served as Class Representative for MCA 2nd Year, which suggests he had both academic and coordination responsibilities.

chunks.title: Bachelor of Computer Applications (BCA), SEMCOM College, Gujarat
chunks.text:
Bachelor of Computer Applications (BCA), SEMCOM College, Gujarat

Mihir completed his BCA at SEMCOM College, Gujarat, from June 2020 to April 2023, graduating with a CGPA of 8.67/10. The CV lists JavaScript, Java, PHP, Python, and AI & Machine Learning among the coursework areas. This stage gave Mihir the programming and software-engineering foundation that later supported both his MCA work and his production web projects.

chunks.title: Experience
chunks.text: Experience

chunks.title: HappinessFactors — Web Developer & Digital Content Editor
chunks.text:
HappinessFactors — Web Developer & Digital Content Editor

Mihir worked with HappinessFactors from January 2025 to November 2025 in a remote freelance capacity. Mihir's work covered delivery and maintenance of the company website, along with digital content editing for promotional material. The portfolio and CV both describe this as a Laravel-based site with Blade templates, Tailwind styling, and Groove.cm integration for headless content management. In practice, that means Mihir handled both the technical side of a maintainable marketing site and the non-code content workflow that allowed the team to update pages without developer intervention.

chunks.title: NTech Infoway — Full-Stack Development Intern
chunks.text:
NTech Infoway — Full-Stack Development Intern

From December 2024 to April 2025, Mihir interned at NTech Infoway and shipped a MERN-stack CRM for Growatt InfoSystem. The work included a React frontend, Express APIs, MongoDB schemas, and role-based views for admin and field-engineer users. The CV also notes Agile teamwork, daily stand-ups, code reviews, reusable dashboard components, form validation, and mobile-friendly UI work. The result was a production CRM that replaced spreadsheet-driven workflows with a single source of truth for client and service records.

chunks.title: Projects
chunks.text: Projects

chunks.title: Smart Office Face Recognition System
chunks.text:
Smart Office Face Recognition System

Mihir's Kingston CI7523 project is a real-time computer-vision access-control prototype built around webcam input. The pipeline combines OpenCV preprocessing, face detection with MTCNN and RetinaFace, and recognition using FaceNet and ArcFace embeddings. The CV extract emphasizes practical robustness work: CLAHE and Gaussian denoising to improve varied-lighting conditions, migration from TensorFlow MTCNN to facenet-pytorch, and standardizing the embedding database to consistent 512D vectors to avoid silent match failures. Mihir wrapped the app in Streamlit, with caching used to reduce rerender overhead and model reloads. What makes the project strong is that it is not just a model demo; it is a complete pipeline with enrollment, thresholding, unknown-face rejection, and a reproducible interface that can be shown to reviewers. It demonstrates both computer-vision depth and deployment awareness.

chunks.title: Multi-Class Classification on Imbalanced Data
chunks.text:
Multi-Class Classification on Imbalanced Data

Mihir's Kingston CI7521 CW1 project benchmarks eight classical classifiers on a five-class imbalanced dataset. The goal was not to chase one model blindly, but to establish a fair and reproducible comparison across LDA, QDA, Decision Tree, KNN, Logistic Regression, SVM, Random Forest, and Gaussian Naive Bayes. The work used a quantile transformer to reduce skew, SMOTE oversampling inside cross-validation folds to avoid leakage, and a two-stage hyperparameter search with RandomizedSearchCV followed by GridSearchCV. Evaluation went beyond raw accuracy and used balanced accuracy, macro and micro one-vs-rest ROC-AUC, and confusion matrices, with macro-F1 used as the main ranking metric. The project is valuable because it shows disciplined experimental design, leakage avoidance, and careful interpretation of class-imbalanced results rather than only a final leaderboard.

chunks.title: Medical Image Classification — OrganSMNIST
chunks.text:
Medical Image Classification — OrganSMNIST

Mihir's Kingston CI7521 CW2 project studies abdominal CT organ classification using OrganSMNIST at 224x224 resolution. Mihir ran 13 controlled experiments across MLPs, custom CNNs, and transfer-learning approaches using MobileNetV2 and ResNet50. The key idea was to compare families of models under consistent splits, augmentation, and training settings so that the outcome reflected architecture choice rather than experiment noise. The CV extract notes a baseline MLP, deeper variants with dropout and batch norm, SGD with momentum, L2 regularization, AdamW, and a data-augmentation variant that reduced overfitting. ResNet50 produced the strongest generalization on the held-out test set. This project shows structured experimentation, a solid grasp of computer-vision training regimes, and comfort with reporting metrics such as accuracy, F1, precision, recall, and confusion matrices in a medical-imaging context.

chunks.title: Growatt CRM — InfoSystem Dashboard
chunks.text:
Growatt CRM — InfoSystem Dashboard

Built during Mihir's NTech Infoway internship, this MERN-stack CRM targeted Growatt InfoSystem’s need to manage clients, service histories, and field-engineer workloads in one place. The portfolio summary describes a React frontend, REST APIs with Node.js and Express, MongoDB schemas, and role-based views for admin and field-engineer users. The application addressed a real operational pain point: the team was previously relying on spreadsheets and paper, which made it difficult to keep a single, accurate source of truth. The project’s strength is in its product thinking. It includes protected routes, JWT auth with role claims, mobile-friendly views for engineers in the field, reusable dashboard components, and a shared validation layer between UI and API. The outcome was a production CRM that replaced fragmented workflows with something maintainable and role-aware, which is the kind of work that translates well into any full-stack role.

chunks.title: HappinessFactors Website
chunks.text:
HappinessFactors Website

Mihir's freelance project delivered a Laravel company website for HappinessFactors. The site used Blade templates and Tailwind CSS for the front end, with Groove.cm integrated as a headless content-management layer so a non-technical team could update pages, posts, and media without touching code. The key problem was not building a pretty brochure site; it was making the site maintainable for the client after handoff. The project therefore focused on server-rendered pages, reusable partials, content-model mapping, and caching choices that balanced freshness with performance. The CV also highlights digital-content work alongside the technical delivery, which means the role was partly engineering and partly operational support for marketing output. The result was a live site that the client can maintain independently, which is a strong signal of practical delivery and not just implementation skill.

chunks.title: Skills
chunks.text:
Skills

Mihir’s strongest area is applied computer vision and deep learning. Mihir lists PyTorch, TensorFlow/Keras, scikit-learn, OpenCV, FaceNet, MTCNN, Streamlit, CNNs, transfer learning, and imbalanced-data techniques such as SMOTE. The CV and portfolio show that this is not just exposure: Mihir has used these tools in coursework projects that involve face recognition, image classification, and comparative ML benchmarking.

On the web and full-stack side, Mihir is comfortable with React.js, Node.js, Express.js, Laravel, MongoDB, MySQL, REST APIs, HTML5, CSS, JavaScript, Blade, Tailwind CSS, and related tooling such as JWT, Axios, React Router, and Mongoose. Mihir's production work at NTech Infoway and HappinessFactors shows that he can work on both frontend and backend concerns.

For general programming, the CV lists Python, JavaScript, Java, PHP, C++, and SQL. Mihir also uses Git, GitHub, VS Code, Jupyter, Postman, and Agile/Scrum practices. The honest summary is that Mihir is strongest in Python for AI/ML and in React/Laravel for web delivery, with working familiarity across the rest of the stack rather than claiming equal depth in everything.

chunks.title: CV Extract
chunks.text:
CV Extract

MIHIR MAKWANA
Computer Vision · Deep Learning · AI
London, UK | +44 7901 037057 | mihirpmakwana786@gmail.com | LinkedIn | GitHub | Portfolio

PROFILE
MSc Artificial Intelligence student at Kingston University with hands-on computer vision experience and a year of full-stack development in production environments. Skilled in PyTorch, FaceNet, MTCNN, and OpenCV through a group Smart Office Face Recognition System project, and in MERN/Laravel stacks through prior internship work. Seeking AI/ML internships, placements, and part-time roles in London — with the engineering background to take models from notebook to deployed product.

EDUCATION
MSc Artificial Intelligence, Kingston University, London Jan 2026 – Jan 2028
Artificial Intelligence with Professional Placement
Master of Computer Applications (MCA), ISTAR College, Gujarat Jul 2023 – May 2025
CGPA: 9.27 / 10 · Class Representative, MCA 2nd Year · Coursework: React, Node.js, Laravel, MongoDB, Python
Bachelor of Computer Applications (BCA), SEMCOM College, Gujarat Jun 2020 – Apr 2023
CGPA: 8.67 / 10 · Coursework: JavaScript, Java, PHP, Python, AI & Machine Learning

TECHNICAL SKILLS
AI / ML PyTorch, TensorFlow / Keras, scikit-learn, OpenCV, FaceNet, MTCNN, Streamlit, Deep Learning, Computer Vision, CNNs, Transfer Learning (MobileNetV2, ResNet50), Feature Engineering, Imbalanced Data (SMOTE)
Programming Python, JavaScript, Java, PHP, C++, SQL
Web / Full-Stack React.js, Node.js, Express.js, Laravel, MongoDB, MySQL, REST APIs, HTML5, CSS
Tools Git, GitHub, VS Code, Jupyter, Postman, Agile / Scrum

PROJECTS
Smart Office Face Recognition System · Kingston University (CI7523) Jan – Apr 2026
Role: Face Detection Specialist · Team of 4
Stack: Python · PyTorch · facenet-pytorch · OpenCV · Streamlit
Multi-Class Classification on Imbalanced Dataset · Kingston University (CI7521 Machine Learning & Deep Learning — CW1) Feb – Mar 2026
Stack: Python · scikit-learn · imbalanced-learn · pandas · matplotlib
Medical Image Classification — OrganSMNIST+ · Kingston University (CI7521 Machine Learning & Deep Learning — CW2) Mar 2026
Stack: Python · TensorFlow / Keras · MedMNIST · MobileNetV2 · ResNet50 · NumPy · Matplotlib

Relevant modules: Computer Vision (CI7523), Deep Learning, Machine Learning, Natural Language Processing, AI Ethics

Key project: Smart Office Face Recognition System — PyTorch, FaceNet, MTCNN, RetinaFace
Built a real-time face recognition system for office access control using MTCNN (facenet-pytorch) and RetinaFace for detection, FaceNet and ArcFace for recognition.
Implemented preprocessing pipeline with CLAHE and Gaussian denoising to improve robustness under varied lighting conditions.
Migrated detection backbone from TensorFlow MTCNN to facenet-pytorch, resolving numpy dtype and Windows Unicode encoding issues.
Standardised embedding database to consistent 512D FaceNet vectors, eliminating silent match failures from mixed dimensions.
Optimised Streamlit demo performance using @st.cache_resource, reducing model reload on every rerun.
Built and compared 8 classifiers (LDA, QDA, Decision Tree, KNN, Logistic Regression, SVM, Random Forest, Gaussian NB) on a 9.8K-sample, 32-feature, 5-class imbalanced dataset (OpenML #4538).
Tuned hyperparameters with RandomizedSearchCV → GridSearchCV; compared scalers (Quantile, Power, Standard) and resampling strategies (SMOTE oversampling / undersampling) to handle class imbalance.
Evaluated using balanced accuracy, macro/micro one-vs-rest ROC-AUC, and confusion matrices; used LinearSVC-based feature selection to improve model generalisation.
Classified abdominal CT organ images from MedMNIST+ OrganSMNIST at 224x224 resolution, benchmarking MLPs, CNNs, and transfer-learning models across 13 experiments in a 4-person team.
Trained a baseline MLP plus 5 improved variants (depth/width, dropout, batch norm, SGD+momentum, L2/AdamW); built baseline and deeper CNNs with matching ablations.
Applied transfer learning with MobileNetV2 and ResNet50 (ImageNet weights); added a data-augmentation variant that reduced overfitting on the best CNN configuration.
Evaluated 6 final models on the held-out test set using accuracy, F1, precision, recall, and confusion matrices — ResNet50 gave the strongest generalisation.

Growatt CRM — Solar InfoSystem Dashboard · NTech Infoway Internship Dec 2024 – Apr 2025
Stack: React.js · Node.js · Express.js · MongoDB
HappinessFactors — Company Website · Freelance Jan 2025 – Nov 2025
Stack: Laravel · PHP · Blade · JavaScript · Tailwind CSS

EXPERIENCE
Web Developer & Digital Content Editor (Freelance), HappinessFactors Jan 2025 – Nov 2025 · Remote
Delivered the company’s Laravel site end-to-end (see Projects) and edited promotional video content to support digital marketing.
Full-Stack Development Intern, NTech Infoway Dec 2024 – Apr 2025 · Remote
Shipped a MERN-stack CRM for Growatt InfoSystem in an Agile team (see Projects) — React frontend, Express APIs, MongoDB schemas, daily stand-ups and code reviews.

Certifications: Introduction to AI · Exploratory Data Analysis for ML · Complete Git & GitHub — Coursera, Geekster
Achievements: 1st Place, Poster Presentation — IGNITE, CHARUSAT (Jan 2024) · Technical Event Coordinator, TECHNOKHOJ-2024
Languages: English (Professional) · Hindi (Native) · Gujarati (Native)
Built React.js frontend for a MERN-stack CRM tracking solar-system clients and service tickets; consumed REST APIs from Node.js/Express backend with MongoDB.
Designed reusable dashboard components, form validation, and role-based views for admin and field-engineer users.
Built and maintained responsive Laravel website with Blade templates and Tailwind-styled frontend; integrated Groove.cm for headless content management.
Optimised cross-platform performance and collaborated with stakeholders on brand-aligned UX.

chunks.title: FAQ
chunks.text: FAQ

chunks.title: Are you available for sponsorship?
chunks.text:
Are you available for sponsorship?

The CV does not state sponsorship status. It only says Mihir is based in London, UK and is seeking AI/ML internships, placements, and part-time roles in London. If a definitive sponsorship answer is needed, it should be confirmed directly with Mihir.

chunks.title: What is your notice period?
chunks.text:
What is your notice period?

The CV does not list a formal notice period. The safest answer is that availability should be confirmed directly with Mihir, especially because Mihir's recent roles include freelance and internship work.

chunks.text:
Where are you based?

Mihir is based in London, UK.

chunks.title: What are you looking for right now?
chunks.text:
What are you looking for right now?

Mihir is currently looking for AI/ML internships, placements, and part-time roles in London, with an emphasis on computer vision, deep learning, and production-minded delivery.

chunks.title: What is your strongest technical area?
chunks.text:
What is your strongest technical area?

Mihir's strongest area is applied AI, especially computer vision and deep learning with Python, PyTorch, OpenCV, FaceNet, MTCNN, TensorFlow/Keras, and Streamlit. Mihir also has practical full-stack experience in React, Node.js, Express, Laravel, and MongoDB.

chunks.title: Can you work end to end?
chunks.text:
Can you work end to end?

Yes. Mihir's portfolio repeatedly shows end-to-end delivery: building models, wiring them into interfaces, and shipping production software or maintainable client websites.
